title: drypetes roxburghii
intro: Drypetes roxburghii, also known as Roxburgh's drypetes or Indian oak, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help in treating various health issues such as fever, cough, and digestive problems. Drypetes roxburghii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
